macro_events.event_summary: Stocks sell off and bond yields spike higher as Trump's diplomatic trip to China fails to resolve a Strait of Hormuz closure/disruption, sustaining an oil supply shock.
primary_drivers: Persistent oil supply disruption from Strait of Hormuz closure (~20% of global oil flows)
Bond rout = higher long-end yields = duration/discount rate hit to long-duration equities
Geopolitical risk premium → safe haven and defense bid

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large US/Permian/Guyana upstream production benefits directly from higher sustained Brent prices; limited Hormuz transit exposure relative to gain on realizations.
Same upstream leverage to higher crude; Permian and US Gulf production unaffected by Hormuz while price decks reprice higher.
Pure-play Permian E&P with high oil beta; every $10 Brent move flows nearly directly to FCF and buyback capacity.
Lockheed is the prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3, and missile systems being deployed/replenished in any Gulf escalation; failed diplomacy increases probability of sustained DoD supplemental.
Northrop benefits from heightened ISR, B-21, and missile defense demand if Hormuz crisis becomes prolonged military standoff.
Cheniere — US LNG exporters become the marginal supplier to Europe/Asia if Middle East oil/gas flows are constrained; Henry Hub-linked contracts widen in value vs. global benchmarks.
Texas Pacific Land — royalty stream on Permian production with zero capex; pure leverage to higher realized oil prices with no operational risk.
TechnipFMC and offshore services see backlog/pricing upside as majors accelerate non-Gulf project FIDs to diversify supply.

losers.rationale: Delta has highest jet fuel exposure as % of opex among US legacy carriers (~25-30%) and limited hedging; crack spreads widening on Hormuz closure hits unit costs directly.
United similarly unhedged on jet fuel with significant trans-Pacific exposure; fuel cost spike + China trip failure suggests trans-Pac demand softness.
Southwest exited fuel hedges; pure spot exposure to distillate spike from Hormuz disruption with no pricing power on point-to-point routes.
Carnival — bunker fuel is a major opex line and consumer discretionary cruise demand wilts in geopolitical/recession scare; high leverage compounds equity hit.
Home Depot — long-duration consumer cyclical hit twice: mortgage rates rise with bond rout (housing turnover slows) and consumer real income squeezed by gasoline.
China trip failure raises probability of retaliatory measures on US firms with large China revenue; Tesla's Shanghai gigafactory and ~22% China revenue exposure most at risk among megacaps.
Boeing — China orderbook (~25% of widebody backlog historically) at risk from failed diplomacy; commercial aviation also pressured by carrier fuel cost spike reducing fleet expansion.

headlines: Stocks tumble, bond rout deepens as Trump’s China visit fails to pry open Strait of Hormuz

macro_events.event_summary: Fed Governor Stephen Miran resigns, with Kevin Warsh expected to take the seat — a personnel shift that markets read as more hawkish/orthodox on the FOMC.
primary_drivers: Shift in FOMC composition toward a more hawkish, market-disciplined voter (Warsh has historically opposed easy policy and QE)
Reduced perceived politicization of the Fed / restored independence signal
Higher-for-longer rate path expectations at the margin

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab's earnings are highly sensitive to short-end rates via NII on client cash sweeps; a more hawkish Fed governor reduces near-term cut odds and supports the deposit spread.

losers.rationale: Zillow and housing-transaction names depend on mortgage rate relief; a hawkish governor swap pushes that further out.
Crypto-linked equities have been bid on debasement/loose-Fed narrative; a Warsh appointment cuts directly against that thesis.

headlines: Fed Governor Stephen Miran resigns for Kevin Warsh

macro_events.event_summary: Top forecasters project US inflation to re-accelerate to 6% in Q2, implying a higher-for-longer rate path and renewed cost pressures.
primary_drivers: Higher discount rate / repricing of Fed cut expectations hurts long-duration assets
Real asset and commodity beneficiaries from inflation hedging flows
Margin compression for companies unable to pass through input costs

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated oil major with direct revenue leverage to crude/refined product prices, which are both a cause of and hedge against the projected inflation print; earnings rise roughly linearly with realized prices.
Same upstream/downstream price exposure as XOM; large-cap energy is the textbook inflation beneficiary and typically sees rotation inflows on hot CPI prints.
Largest US-listed gold miner; gold tends to bid on stagflation fears and real-rate uncertainty when inflation reaccelerates faster than nominal yields.
Pure-play US E&P with high oil-price torque and Buffett sponsorship; cash flow scales directly with WTI which is a key CPI component driver.
Self-storage REIT with short-duration leases (monthly repricing) — one of the few REIT subsectors that can re-rate rents in real time to inflation, unlike long-lease office/triple-net peers.
Nitrogen fertilizer producer; food/agricultural input prices typically lead the inflation basket and CF has direct pricing power on urea/ammonia.

losers.rationale: Deep-discount retailer serving low-income consumers who are most squeezed by food/energy inflation; also faces direct SG&A wage and freight cost pressure with limited pricing flexibility on fixed price points.
Consumer staples with commodity input exposure (pulp, resins) and already-stretched price elasticity after multi-year hikes; further pass-through risks volume declines.
Realty Income — long-duration net-lease REIT with fixed rent escalators (~1-2%) well below 6% inflation; bond-proxy that suffers as 10Y yield backs up.
BNPL lender whose funding costs rise and whose subprime consumer borrower base is the first to crack under sustained 6% inflation; double squeeze on net interest margin and credit losses.

headlines: Inflation rate projected to hit 6% in the second quarter, top economic forecasters say

macro_events.event_summary: A MarketWatch opinion piece frames Fed Chair Powell as potentially losing his battles against both inflation and political pressure from Trump, raising concerns about Fed independence and stickier inflation.
primary_drivers: Erosion of Fed independence risk premium pushes long-end yields higher and steepens curve
Persistent/sticky inflation expectations support hard-asset hedges
Policy uncertainty weakens USD and benefits non-dollar stores of value

beneficiaries.rationale: Newmont is the largest US-listed gold miner with operational leverage to rising gold prices — every $100/oz gold move flows roughly 2-3x to earnings given fixed cost base.
Integrated oil benefits from sticky inflation thesis and serves as real-asset/cash-flow hedge; energy is the historical winner when Fed is perceived behind the curve.

headlines: Powell’s legacy as Fed chair is fighting inflation and Trump. He may lose the battle against both.

macro_events.event_summary: Equities sell off as oil prices and Treasury yields jump simultaneously, with mega-cap AI names (Nvidia, Cerebras) leading declines.
primary_drivers: Higher long-duration yields compress multiples on high-duration growth/AI equities
Rising crude lifts energy producer cash flows and reflation trades
Risk-off rotation out of crowded AI momentum names into energy and short-duration value

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with upstream leverage; ~every $10/bbl move in Brent flows directly to free cash flow. Direct beneficiary of the oil price jump cited in headline.
Pure-play Permian E&P with high oil beta (no gas dilution, low hedging). Among the highest-torque large-cap names to a crude rally.
Oilfield services leader — higher sustained oil prices drive upstream capex and rig activity, second-derivative beneficiary.
Large-cap pure E&P with significant US shale and global LNG exposure; direct cash flow leverage to the crude move.
Money-center bank with the most asset-sensitive balance sheet among large peers; rising long yields steepen the curve and lift NII guidance.

losers.rationale: Explicitly named in headline as sliding. High-duration AI bellwether — rising yields compress the terminal-value multiple on the most crowded long in the market.
AI-adjacent, premium-multiple semis name with high duration; moves in sympathy with NVDA on yield-driven multiple compression.
AI server pure-play with high beta to NVDA and the AI capex narrative; suffers in tandem on AI momentum unwind plus yield headwind.
Jet fuel is ~25-30% of opex; crude spike directly compresses margin and airlines historically the cleanest short on oil rallies.
Domestic carrier with limited fuel hedging post-2022; near-pure exposure to jet fuel cost on a crude rally.
Largest US homebuilder; mortgage rates track 10Y yields, so a yield jump directly pressures affordability and order volumes.

headlines: Stock Market Today: Dow Falls As Oil Prices, Yields Jump; Nvidia, Cerebras Slide (Live Coverage)
